summary: Soundboard recording with very good clarity (a little thin on the highs) captures a stellar Spring ’77 show highlighted by scorching “Scarlet > Fire”, a gorgeous “High Time”, and an explosive Terrapin‑Station jam.
review: The SBD source is clean and well balanced, though reviewers note it could use a touch more high‑frequency sparkle. Musically the concert shines – Jerry’s near‑perfect Travis Bean tone, Donna’s standout vocal on “Jack‑A‑Roe”, and a second‑set Terrapin > Playing > Wharf Rat sequence that many fans call legendary. Overall the performance is rated five stars by the community with only minor recording nit‑picks.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently 5‑star show comments (≈70% weight) and a solid 4‑star recording assessment (≈30% weight) yield an overall rating near 4.7.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear overall; reviewers praise the mix but mention slightly lacking highs and a faint hiss between tracks that can be tamed by modest treble boost.

standout_songs: Terrapin Station > Playing > Wharf Rat > Playing
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with a sweet “High Time” and strong vocal moments; the second set erupts into a classic Terrapin jam chain and ends with a high‑energy Sugar Mag encore that lets Bobby’s drumming shine.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Near‑perfect Travis Bean tone praised, though some note the aluminum neck sounds slightly tinny compared to later Wolf guitar tones.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is described as loose and independent, adding a relaxed vibe on keyboards.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bobby (the drummer) “loses it” in the Sugar Mag encore, indicating high energy playing.
